title: Unidentified Red Nebula species
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Unidentified species}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}
{{Species
|image=[[File:Red_Nebula_dude.jpg]]

|designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]<ref name="SW50">[[Star Wars (1977) 50|''Star Wars'' (1977) 50]].</ref>

A '''sentient species''' inhabited a [[Planet of the Red Nebula|planet]] in the [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], that was slowly drifting away from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] due to a [[Awesome cataclysm|cosmic cataclysm]]. They were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] beings who resembled [[Human/Legends|Humans]] with [[Color/Legends|pink]] or pale skin and large, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] were deeply religious, and followed a faith centered on a pair of gems known as the [[Great Life Jewels]]. According to the species' legends, the jewels came to their world during an cataclysm that created the Red Nebula and forced it to drift away from the galaxy, taking their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] with it. The gems, the legend claimed, shielded a [[Untouched Valley|valley]] on their [[Planet/Legends|planet]] from the destruction wrought by the [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] on other celestial bodies within it. However, rather than unifying the species' culture, the belief split them into rival factions. One side, represented by an order of crimson-robed [[Priests of the Red Nebula|priests]], considered the Great Life Gems a boon to civilization and thus venerated the stones. Their rivals, however, believed that the gems had thwarted fate, and kept the species alive when it should have been destroyed. The nihilist group was eventually cast off the planet and forced to roam the galaxy as [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiles]].

In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the [[Red Nebula exiles|outcast faction]], under the leadership of [[Klysk]], kidnapped [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]] from the galactic fringe to mount a [[Attack on the Red Nebula temple|raid]] on the [[Red Nebula temple|temple]] where the gems were kept. Klysk hoped to steal the jewels and destroy them, thus hastening what his faction believed to be the species' true destiny: extinction. One of their abductees was the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who managed to pilfer the jewels from the temple and then con Klysk into taking a sackful of rocks into the [[Red Nebula system|system's]] [[Sun of the Planet of the Red Nebula|sun]] instead of the jewels. [[3 ABY/Legends|Three years later]], [[Domina Tagge/Legends|Domina Tagge]] led a [[Mission to the Red Nebula|mission]] to the world to steal the gems in a bid to spread a [[Crimson Forever|plague]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. She razed the species' main city and removed the gems.

==Biology and appearance==
The members of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]] [[Species/Legends|species]] looked similar to [[Human/Legends|Humans]] in most respects. They had [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] bodies, five-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]], and faces that featured two small [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a nose, and a mouth. They differed from Humans in that their [[Bone/Legends|craniums]] were bald, and they had large, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. Some members of the species had pale-[[Color/Legends|colored]] flesh, while others had pink skin. At least one member of the species had sharp nails on his fingers.<ref name="SW50" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|We temple priests have seen the Great Life Jewels tempt many. Legend says the jewels fell to this planet during the awesome cataclysm which severed our cluster of the galaxy from the main body and created the Red Nebula. It is our belief that the gems' aura protects us from the destructive fate which seemed our destiny from the time of the cataclysm.|Temple priest, to Han Solo|<ref name="SW50" />}}
[[File:Klysk.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Klysk led his species' nihilist faction during the Galactic Civil War.]]
The pointy-eared humanoids were native to a [[Planet of the Red Nebula|planet]] located within the Red Nebula,<ref name="SW50" /> an astrological feature far from the main body of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> located in a [[Red Nebula comet field|field]] of [[Comet/Legends|comets]], [[Asteroid/Legends|meteors]], and other navigational hazards. Their [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was routinely barraged by debris, but one [[Untouched Valley|valley]] was spared the impacts due to the provenance of a pair of vermillion gems<ref name="SW50" /> known as the [[Great Life Jewels]]<ref name="CSWE III 87">{{CSWECite|III|87}}.</ref> or the Twin Gems. Because it was the only safe area on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the species lived in a [[City in the Untouched Valley|city]] in the valley and guarded the gems in a domed [[Red Nebula temple|temple]]. This structure was monumental in scale, built with huge columns with carvings along their plinths and with an arched entrance. The Life Jewels were kept high atop a pair of pillars.<ref name="SW50" />

Although their technology was underdeveloped compared to that of much of the galaxy, the species did have access to some advanced items. For instance, they knew how to use [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking devices]], which they could monitor from [[fineband scope]] viewscreens. They also maintained a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] that were capable of leaving the Red Nebula.<ref name="SW50" /> The species' home planet appeared on no [[Star map/Legends|star-charts]],<ref name="CSWE III 87" /> and it was a long distance from the next [[Star system/Legends|star system]] by [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Nevertheless, the species did receive the occasional visitor. From such contacts, at least some members of the species learned to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]].<ref name="SW50" />

The members of the Red Nebula species were highly religious. However, they were divided into two rival religious factions. One group, represented by an order of [[Priests of the Red Nebula|priests]] distinguished by their crimson robes, believed that the Life Jewels were holy artifacts that protected their society from destruction and allowed them to live in harmony with the Red Nebula. The other [[Red Nebula exiles|faction]], led by a member of the species known as [[Klysk]] around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], held that the Life Jewels were abominations that only forestalled the destiny of the species: to be destroyed by the Red Nebula. Both groups agreed that the gems were alive and had come from another universe during the [[awesome cataclysm]] that had created the Red Nebula; if the gems were ever separated, the belief went, they would become angry and harm those around them. The members of the latter group wished to destroy the Twin Gems to allow the species to meet its predestined fate. They were considered fanatics and unbelievers and forbidden to live on the species' homeworld by the priestly group.<ref name="SW50" />

The faction led by the priests tended to the jewels and defended the temple from thieves with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. Their protection also prevented the Twin Gems from being separated, a situation that would cause both gems to inflict a plague known as the [[Crimson Forever|crimson forever]]. Although the clerics carried no personal weapons in the event an intruder made it into the temple, they were aided in their sentry duty by a giant, hairy [[Red Nebula temple guardian's species|creature]] who served as the [[Red Nebula temple guardian|temple guardian]] and attacked intruders. The priests also had access to a series of underground shelters throughout the valley.<ref name="SW50" />

The doomsday faction traveled through the galaxy in a starship called the ''[[Nova Prince]]''. In contrast to the priests' proclaimed non-violence, the doomsday faction had no compunction about using force as a means to an end. At least one member of the group wore a green tunic with a belt around the waist over basic leggings. The members of the doomsday faction were able to speak Basic.<ref name="SW50" />

==History==
{{Quote|Ancestral legend has it that this nebula was once an arm of your galaxy…until cosmic upheaval sundered it. Now it drifts further and further into the void…only this star and this planet surviving against all odds.|Klysk|<ref name="SW50" />}}
[[File:Red_nebula_priest.jpg|thumb|right|upright|An order of robed priests emerged to lead one faction of the species.]]
Although they evolved on the single habitable planet located in the Red Nebula,<ref name="CSWE III 87" /> the species' legends told of a time long before when their [[Red Nebula system|star system]] was part of the main body of the galaxy. They believed that a tumultuous cataclysm had created the Red Nebula, simultaneously casting it into the depths of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and taking their [[Sun of the Planet of the Red Nebula|sun]] and planet with it. A religion developed among the species that claimed that only the blessing of the Great Life Jewels protected them from the same destruction as the other astral bodies located in the [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="SW50" />

Despite their isolation, the species' world received occasional visits from offworlders,<ref name="SW50" /> and it came to be considered part of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="TEA" /> At least some members of the species learned to speak Basic from those encounters, and the visitors' frequent attempts to steal the Great Life Jewels prompted the species to set up defenses to protect their treasures. The species also achieved some elements of high technology, such as spaceflight. They constructed a small fleet of vessels and considered fleeing their world to settle elsewhere, but in the end, they had too few ships to carry all members of the species.<ref name="SW50" />

Over time, a fissure developed among the members of the species. Some individuals maintained that the red gems were sacred and had to be venerated and protected if the species was to continue to live in the Red Nebula. Others, however, believed that the jewels were diabolical and only prevented the species from meeting the fate meted out to them by destiny: to die in the Red Nebula. The former faction gained the upper hand, and the nihilists were [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|banished]] from the world in a small ship.<ref name="SW50" />

===Theft of the Great Life Gems===
{{Quote|Take any plunder you wish during the attack…it is yours to keep. All except the two great gems which lie in the temple…those you will bring to me or my companions on the ship. And at all costs…the jewels must be kept together. Do not separate them…on pain of instant ionization!|Klysk|<ref name="SW50" />}}
[[File:Battle_of_red_nebula.jpg|thumb|left|Klysk brought a cadre of mercenaries to steal the Great Life Jewels.]]
The exiles lived in the greater galaxy for [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. They eventually obtained a [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] called the ''Nova Prince'' and then set into motion a plan to bring about the annihilation of their species. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], under the leadership of Klysk, they kidnapped several [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], with at least one raid on the planet [[Terminus/Legends|Terminus]]. The ''Nova Prince'' then headed for the Red Nebula, where Klysk informed the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] that they had been kidnapped so that they could plunder the temple and steal the Great Life Jewels.<ref name="SW50" />

However, navigating the debris of the Red Nebula proved difficult for the exiles, so they enlisted the aid of one of their captives, a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler named [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. He succeeded, and the exiles returned home at last. They marched the captives to the valley, where their city with the temple was located. Klysk ordered them to [[Attack on the Red Nebula temple|attack]] the temple and allowed them to keep any plunder they grabbed as long as the gems were brought to him.<ref name="SW50" />

The priests defended the temple with laser cannons and took out several of the marauders. Nevertheless, Han Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], made it to the temple, followed shortly by a scoundrel named [[Zud]] and two of his companions. The temple priests warned them to turn back, but Zud refused. The giant temple guardian rampaged into the chamber and dispatched Zud and his helpers only to become infatuated with Chewbacca. Solo took advantage of the disturbance to steal the gems.<ref name="SW50" />

Meanwhile, Klysk awaited the outcome of the raid at the ''Nova Prince''. When Solo appeared with a sack in his hand, Klysk intimidated the Human into handing it over. Outgunned, the smuggler acquiesced. Klysk collected the remaining scoundrels and members of his faction—leaving Solo and Chewbacca behind to punish the Corellian for trying to negotiate with him. Klysk then sped away in the ''Prince'' on a suicide course for the system's sun, to destroy the gems and finally bring about his species' fated destruction.<ref name="SW50" />

The temple priests were convinced that they were doomed; as they and Solo watched Klysk's ship from an underground viewscreen, the ''Nova Prince'' burnt up in the fires of the sun. Solo then revealed that he had bluffed Klysk by giving him a sack of rocks; the real gems were hidden in the forest. With the smuggler's help, the priests recovered the sacred objects and placed them back on the pillars on which they belonged. In gratitude, the priests gave Solo and Chewbacca a ship with which to return to the main body of the galaxy.<ref name="SW50" />

===Death from above===
{{Quote|And when spies brought me rumors of two strange, deadly gems in the Red Nebula…I rushed to investigate. As our ship laid waste to the primitive civilization there, driving off some giant creature…this elite group of bounty hunters I'd hired captured the jewels and the priest tending them.|Domina Tagge|<ref name="SW50" />}}
[[File:TaggeCo_customer_service.jpg|thumb|right|Domina Tagge's forces laid waste to the sole inhabited city on the planet in the Red Nebula.]]
Despite this brush with annihilation, the species had only a brief reprieve: in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Human [[Noble/Legends|noble]] [[Domina Tagge/Legends|Domina Tagge]] [[Mission to the Red Nebula|led]] a [[Warship/Legends|warship]] full of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to the species' planet to steal the Twin Gems. Tagge had heard that the gems were powerful, and she hoped to turn their power against both the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor's]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] hero, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], both of whom she blamed for the death of her brother, [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]]. The inhabitants of the valley city stood little chance against the ship's [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]], and the settlement was completely wiped out, the gems stolen, and the temple priest captured.<ref name="SW50" />

Tagge had the priest interrogated, and he eventually revealed the secrets of the gems to her: they had the power to infect the crimson forever plague. However, she had been followed by an [[Unidentified Imperial-class Star Destroyer (Crimson Forever)|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] sent by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader. As [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] from the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] boarded Tagge's vessel to retrieve the gems for themselves, the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters provoked a firefight, allowing the Imperial forces to only get one of the gems. During the fracas, the temple priest released the other gem from its casing. When the container holding Tagge's stolen gem burst open per a booby-trap set by Tagge, it was released on the Imperial vessel. Both gems were thus free to inflict the plague for which they were feared: the crimson forever.<ref name="SW50" />

==The Red Nebula species in the galaxy==
{{Quote|We are exiles…returning home! Returning from years in an alien environment! Returning to make right the great wrong done when we were driven away!|Klysk|<ref name="SW50" />}}
The members of this species maintained a small group of ships for trips into the main galaxy. The exiled faction that sought to destroy the Great Life Gems roamed the galaxy for years, eventually gaining possession of the ''Nova Prince''.<ref name="SW50" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Kraaken_swenc.jpg|thumb|right|Kraaken appears in ''Traitor's Gambit'', a later story by Goodwin and Williamson.]]
The Red Nebula species made their debut in [[Star Wars (1977) 50|''Star Wars'' (1977) 50]], an issue of the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series that was first published in [[August]] [[1981]]. The issue was written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]]. The species is also referenced in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE III 87" /> Goodwin and Williamson later created [[Kraaken]], a character whose appearance was identical to the inhabitants of the Red Nebula, for the comic strip story arc "[[Traitor's Gambit]]", published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] from [[January]] to [[March]] [[1982]]. ''Traitor's Gambit'' does not name the character's species.<ref name="TG">"[[Traitor's Gambit]]"</ref>

However, identifying Kraaken as a member of the species from the Red Nebula is difficult; indeed, ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Canon|continuity]] consultant [[Leland Chee]] has stated on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]] that his theory is that all pointy-eared characters that have no explicit species identification are likely [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]],<ref name="TT">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM}}</ref> a species introduced on the ''[[Jedi: Yoda]]'' comic book in [[2004]].<ref name="JY">''[[Jedi: Yoda]]''</ref> Chee went further, stating his hypothesis that "all pointy-eared species are either [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] or closely related to Sephi."<ref name="TT" /> However, ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' states that the inhabitants of the Red Nebula evolved on their homeworld,<ref name="CSWE III 87" /> complicating any simple identification of Kraaken as either a member of that species or a Sephi.<ref name="TG" />

''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', published in [[2009]], indicates that despite the loss of the Twin Gems and Domina Tagge's leveling of the main city of the Red Nebula's only habitable world, the system had ''some'' sentient denizens—albeit fewer than one million—in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. However, the ''Atlas'' does not specify whether these were members of the pointy-eared species.<ref name="TEA" />
